concessions.ask: Hold card take rate at 0.85% (+ SLA)

plan_b_scenarios.ai_analysis: This is the highest-probability failure mode. Carlos was hired 6 months ago with an explicit cost-reduction mandate from the board post-Series A. His rejection will not be about price — it will be a signal that his credibility at FlexiPay depends on visible cost wins. The counter must give him a win without ceding the strategic frame.
response_plan: Escalate to Yuno CEO → Sofia Alvarez within 48 hours. Frame as strategic partnership, not vendor negotiation.
Offer Carlos a 'CFO Dashboard' with monthly performance SLA reporting — gives him visible accountability vs dLocal's opacity.
Introduce a tiered pricing structure: 0.85% on first $15M/mo, 0.78% on TPV above — ties Yuno's discount to growth Carlos wants anyway.
Deliver a written risk memo to FlexiPay's board (with Sofia's permission) on dLocal commitment trap and tokenization migration risk.

plan_b_scenarios.trigger: 90-day approval-rate sprint misses targets in Peru
plan_b_scenarios.ai_analysis: Peru is structurally hard: 2 acquirers, small issuer base, limited routing optionality. If we miss PE targets, the SLA exposes Yuno financially AND gives dLocal a 'told you so' narrative. The key is to set PE targets conservatively upfront AND have a contingency acquirer integration ready.
response_plan: Pre-negotiate a 3rd Peru acquirer integration (Niubiz or Izipay) ready to deploy within 30 days of first SLA miss.
Structure the SLA with market-specific floors: MX 82%, CO 80%, CL 80%, PE 75% — not a single blended number.
Add a 'rolling 90-day' measurement window to avoid single-month noise triggering SLA credits.
Set aside $50K reserve for PE-specific acquirer onboarding fees in the Q2 budget.

plan_b_scenarios.trigger: dLocal drops card take rate to 0.60% or lower
plan_b_scenarios.ai_analysis: This is the 'race to the bottom' scenario. If dLocal drops to 0.60%, they are buying the account at a loss — which tells you everything about how strategically they see FlexiPay. Do NOT match. Matching resets Yuno's entire LatAm pricing floor for every other customer. Instead, compete on structural differentiation.
response_plan: Do NOT drop below 0.80% on cards. This is a hard floor — message from VP to sales team.
Shift the narrative entirely to performance-based commercial terms: 'pay for outcomes, not volume'.
Introduce a success-fee structure: 0.85% base + rebate of 5 bps per percentage point of approval rate improvement above a 82% floor. Aligns incentives, quantifies the value.

Expose dLocal's unit economics publicly: if they're selling at 0.60%, they're losing money on FlexiPay, which means service quality will erode within 12 months. Get this into Daniela's hands as internal intel.

plan_b_scenarios.trigger: FlexiPay delays renewal decision past 45-day window
plan_b_scenarios.ai_analysis: Counter-intuitively, this helps us. Yuno's contract auto-renews unless 60-day notice is given. If FlexiPay misses the window, we get another year by default. The risk is they give notice as a negotiation tactic — so we need to frame urgency without creating a hostile countdown.
response_plan: Quietly confirm internally with Daniela that the 60-day notice window has passed (if current date > T-60 days from March 15 renewal).
Do NOT mention the auto-renew clause proactively — it removes their incentive to close.
Schedule a 'Brazil readiness review' for day 30 that forces a commitment decision (they can't launch BR without vendor certainty).
Prepare to offer a 60-day grace period on renewal signing IF they commit to Brazil fast-track — decouples the two decisions.

plan_b_scenarios.ai_analysis: Low probability but catastrophic. Daniela is the only stakeholder actively defending Yuno. If she leaves, her replacement may be hired BY Carlos with a cost mandate. We need to broaden the champion network now, not reactively.
response_plan: Immediately identify 2nd-tier champions within FlexiPay engineering: the 4 backend devs. They feel the support quality directly.
Schedule a 'Yuno Engineering Open House' — Yuno Solutions Eng + FlexiPay devs, no KAM present. Builds ground-level relationships.
Document every deliverable in writing with Daniela CC'd, so a successor has evidence of Yuno value without Daniela's verbal endorsement.
If Daniela leaves, extend her a Yuno advisor role offer within 48 hours — preserves relationship + signals to market.

plan_b_scenarios.trigger: FlexiPay signs with dLocal — account lost
plan_b_scenarios.ai_analysis: The ultimate failure mode. If it happens, the goal shifts from retention to maximum strategic value extraction: preserve relationship, turn the loss into pipeline intelligence, and create a win-back path.
response_plan: Offer a 6-month 'safety net' clause: 30-day pre-approved path back to Yuno at original pricing if dLocal underperforms. Creates a credible win-back trigger.
Turn FlexiPay's 18-month track record into a public case study (with their permission) — fuels the 20+ BNPL prospects in LatAm pipeline.
Extract detailed post-mortem data from Daniela on WHY they switched — use it to harden the next 5 renewals across the portfolio.
Schedule quarterly check-ins with Daniela personally — she came from Mercado Pago, she'll move again. This is a 5-year relationship, not an 18-month one.
If FlexiPay's BR launch is delayed because of the switch, Yuno uses it publicly (carefully) as evidence that orchestration speed matters. Turn their mistake into pipeline.

qbr_scripts.tone: Bottom-line first. Zero fluff. Spreadsheets and numbers.
qbr_scripts.opening_hook: Carlos, before anything else — the dLocal savings number is actually $427,920, not $370K. I want you to have the right number on the table.
three_bullets: dLocal's $427K headline savings destroy $707K of FlexiPay value in Year 1 once you price in 160 eng hours, 2-month Brazil delay, 2-point approval drop, token migration, and the $4.3M/mo commitment shortfall you're exposed to on their 24-month term.

Your current Yuno spend is 0.83% of TPV — that's the median of the LatAm orchestration market, not the top. The real benchmarking question isn't 'is 0.85% expensive', it's 'what does 0.67% buy you?' — and the answer is: a single-acquirer setup in a market where your Peru routing is already the weakest link.

Here's what I'm authorized to give you: platform fee waived ($24K/yr), PIX matched to 0.50% on a 24-month commit, performance SLA with approval-rate floors, and 18-month term with no hard TPV minimum. Total concession value: ~$75K. Your line-item wins, your Brazil ships on time, and you don't inherit dLocal's commitment trap. Sign it, and Q1 goes home quiet.
qbr_scripts.close: I can have this in writing by end of day tomorrow. What do you need from me to green-light it?

qbr_scripts.tone: Data-driven, technical, hates fluff. Show your work.
qbr_scripts.opening_hook: Daniela, I built the case you asked me for. Here's the one-line version: the switching math doesn't work for FlexiPay at any price, and I can back every number.
three_bullets: Your January TPV dip is seasonal — proportional across all 4 markets, support tickets flat, 54% 6-month trailing growth intact. Don't let Carlos read the dip as a platform problem. It's not.

Your real performance issue is Peru and Colombia routing — you're 4.3 points under benchmark in CO and 7.3 under in PE. That's $6.1M/yr of FlexiPay GMV being declined at checkout and $305K of net revenue you're leaving on the table. I'm committing to a 90-day sprint to close those gaps, with market-specific SLAs backing it.

Brazil goes live in 3 weeks on Yuno vs 6-8 weeks on dLocal. Your 4-dev team doesn't have 1,280 engineering hours to spend re-integrating. Switching costs you the Brazil launch Sofia promised the board — and switching cost itself is $1.13M Year 1 vs $427K headline savings. I need you co-presenting this with me to Carlos.
qbr_scripts.close: Will you co-sign the narrative in tomorrow's QBR? I'll hand you the deck. You hand Carlos the math.

qbr_scripts.tone: Big picture. Competitive positioning. How does this help us win?
qbr_scripts.opening_hook: Sofia, this decision isn't about payments. It's about whether FlexiPay ships Brazil in Q1 or not.
three_bullets: You said 2025 is your Brazil year and first-mover advantage matters. Yuno already supports PIX, Boleto, and local Brazilian acquiring on your existing integration — 3 weeks to live. dLocal needs a full re-integration: 6-8 weeks, minimum. That's 2 months of first-mover advantage you cannot get back. Your competitors ship while you're re-plumbing.

Under our Base case, Brazil + Argentina generate $1.24M of net FlexiPay revenue in Year 1 — 2.9× the dLocal headline savings. The decision isn't 'save $427K on a line item'. It's 'capture $1.24M of new market or don't'. I have the model in front of me.

Yuno is asking for an executive call with you directly — our CEO to you, in the next 14 days. This is positioned as a strategic partnership for FlexiPay's Series B expansion, not a vendor renewal. Your Head of Payments trusts us, your CFO has a cost lens, and you break ties. We want you to break them with the Brazil lens, not the line-item lens.
qbr_scripts.close: Can we put the executive call on the calendar this week?
